Full job description
Head of Data Strategy

Location: Finance and Performance Directorate

Permanent contract

Full time – 37.5 hours per week

Hybrid role with home/office working in Matlock

£65,000 per annum

A fantastic opportunity has arisen to join our Finance and Performance Directorate as Head of Data Strategy.

The purpose of the role is to establish YHA's data strategy and architecture to support digital transformation and our long-term organisational transformation plans. Working closely with the Executive Director Resources & Transformation and Director of Finance & Performance, you'll build data governance frameworks, enable self-service analytics capabilities, and support the cultural shift toward data-driven decision making across YHA. You'll provide technical leadership for data transformation while building capability across the organisation.

Why work for YHA?

Join our team and enjoy a range of exclusive staff benefits that support your well-being and career growth:

10 nights free hostel stays per year for you and up to 3 friends or family

Access to YHA’s staff discount and cash back portal

Free YHA Membership each year

Access to support via our Employee Assistance Program

There are many more benefits to working for YHA so please visit our YHA Jobs website for more information.

What will you be doing as Head of Data Strategy

Working with the Finance and Performance Team you will:

Lead the development and delivery of YHA’s data strategy and future data architecture, ensuring our systems and reporting platforms work seamlessly together to support digital transformation. You’ll establish data governance frameworks, set organisation‑wide data standards, and oversee the evolution of our business intelligence capabilities—including shaping dashboards and reporting tools that drive better decision-making.

Play a key role in enabling cultural change across YHA by improving data capability and literacy, creating practical tools for non‑technical users, and building directorate‑level data champions. Through strategic coaching and insight, you’ll help senior leaders embed data into their decisions and highlight opportunities to accelerate data‑driven ways of working.

Lead the consolidation of analytical functions, streamline tools and processes, and shape the future structure of YHA’s analytics capability—initially managing the Performance Improvement Manager and BI Developer and preparing the organisation for post‑transformation needs.

What Skills and Experience do you need to be a Head of Data Strategy

To work as part of the Finance and Performance Team we are looking for candidates with the following qualities:

Significant experience leading data transformation in complex, multi‑site environments, along with strong technical expertise across data architecture, governance, SQL, and Power BI. Experience with hospitality systems—particularly Mews—or wider system migration programmes will be a strong advantage.

Ability to communicate complex ideas simply, influence senior stakeholders, and drive cultural change toward data‑driven decision making. You’ll have a track record of consolidating analytical functions, managing technology‑enabled transformation, and ideally an understanding of charity sector reporting. Above all, you’ll bring strong stakeholder skills and demonstrate our HEART values in the way you work.

For more information about the role and the skills and experience required, please refer to the job description.

At YHA, we understand the richness of life experiences, and we place just as much importance on lived experiences as we do on skills and knowledge. We provide a robust induction, ongoing training, and opportunities for personal growth. While certain roles may require specific skills, we welcome applicants from all backgrounds. For more information about how we are creating an inclusive workplace please visit our jobs website.

Who are YHA?

YHA is a social enterprise with a mission to enrich the lives of all, especially young people. YHA does this by providing brilliant hostel stays and experiences that improve physical health, mental wellbeing and life skills.

If you would like to find out more about YHA the charity, the culture, the heritage and so much more please visit our jobs website.

How do you apply?

Submit your CV and complete the application form. For more information about our recruitment process, visit the jobs website. If you need any assistance, please email jobs@yha.org.uk

Once the hiring manager has reviewed your application we will contact shortlisted candidates. We aim to get in touch within a reasonable amount of time after the closing date. You can also check on the progress of your application if you have signed up to Connect - our online candidate portal

If you require reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please get in touch by emailing jobs@yha.org.uk.

PLEASE NOTE All relevant checks are in place to ensure safeguarding and legal requirements of safer recruitment are adhered to. We do reserve the right to close this advertisement early if we receive a high volume of suitable applications.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My reading of this role is that it needs someone who can set a clear data direction, then make it real through governance, reporting, capability building and stakeholder buy-in. It also needs someone who can simplify the technical side for non-technical colleagues and help shift habits, not just systems.

focusAreasToBring.title: Clear governance

focusAreasToBring.title: Practical analytics

focusAreasToBring.title: Capability building
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would spend time with senior leaders and directorates to build confidence, not just compliance, around using data in decisions.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a mix of operational leadership, reporting discipline and change delivery that fits a role like this well. My background is not narrow data-specialist work alone; it is the combination of structure, stakeholder management and practical improvement that makes the difference in complex environments.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first months clarifying the current data landscape, tightening priorities and helping leaders see where the biggest reporting and governance gaps are. From there, I’d focus on making analytics more usable, reducing duplication and building confidence across the organisation so the strategy becomes part of day-to-day decision-making.
companyHighlights: YHA England & Wales is a social enterprise and charity with a mission to enrich lives, especially young people.
The role sits in the Finance and Performance Directorate.
The organisation offers hybrid working and a package of staff benefits including hostel stays and membership.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments that value purpose, clarity and steady improvement. The emphasis on lived experience, training and personal growth feels like a good match for the way I like to work with people and build capability.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would map the existing data, reporting and governance landscape, meet the key stakeholders and understand where decisions are being slowed down or distorted by poor visibility.
I would start shaping the immediate data priorities, identify duplication or weak points in reporting, and work with the team on clearer standards and practical tools.
I would begin bedding in the first improvements to dashboards, governance and ways of working, while supporting leaders and data champions to use the new approach consistently.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring a steady, practical approach to helping YHA make better decisions from data and build capability that lasts.
closingProofPoints: I improved NHS primary care Google reviews from 2.09 to 4.51.
I built senior reporting across workflow, utilisation, payroll and income, including reconciliation of 64 PCSE remittance files.
I led cross-site improvement work across five major European theme park sites.
contentNotes: I have not claimed direct SQL or Power BI delivery beyond the CV’s stated skills because the evidence bank does not provide detailed project examples for those tools.
I have not referenced Mews or charity-sector reporting experience directly because I do not have evidence for either.
I have kept the company section limited to facts clearly stated in the advert.
